page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmc9ae.48xlarge is a bare-metal class instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing AMD EPYC 9T95 processors running at a base frequency of 2.25 GHz. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-optimized allocation of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a high-capacity baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The absence of hyperthreading, indicated by a single thread per physical core, ensures dedicated execution resources per vCPU, which minimizes resource contention but limits concurrent thread scaling compared to SMT-enabled systems.

The ecs.ebmc9ae.48xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities and dedicated CPU execution. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly cost-efficient for raw mathematical modeling, high-performance computing, large-scale simulation, and heavy compilation tasks where memory capacity is secondary to raw CPU throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmc9ae.48xlarge is a compute-optimized bare-metal instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9T95 processors running at 2.25 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with a KVM hypervisor, it provides 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but offers a robust 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. With a 1:1 mapping of physical cores to vCPUs and no hyperthreading, it eliminates resource contention for demanding workloads. This configuration is optimized for high-performance computing, parallel processing, scientific simulations, and compute-heavy tasks where dedicated CPU execution is critical.
